rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by using only visible identifiers from the current component scope; do not invent new declarations.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or property references not present in the model.
Ensure type consistency in AGREE expressions: numeric variables must have numeric expressions, Booleans must have Boolean expressions.
Move implementation-level bindings to the correct implementation annex and avoid placing them in component type contracts.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, always validate that referenced identifiers exist in the current scope.
AGREE expressions must preserve type correctness and avoid unsafe conversions between data types.
Component type contracts should only reference features visible at that scope; implementation-level details belong in the implementation annex.
Avoid encoding AADL property associations (e.g., Stack_Size) as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed in the model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use only declared constants, equations, or visible ports in AGREE expressions.
Helper functions must be defined within the same annex or imported from a package.
Ensure that all references in AGREE clauses are resolvable within the AADL model.
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture elements or declarations when fixing AGREE diagnostics.
Avoid referencing subcomponent ports or implementation-level paths in component type annexes.
Do not attempt to resolve AADL property names (e.g., Stack_Size) as AGREE variables.
Do not convert Boolean or opaque data types into numeric expressions without explicit scalar representation.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
AGREE validator diagnostics about annex placement
Model Analyst Agent tasks related to AADL structure extraction
